source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple sessions in same cwd
notes: Two concurrent claude REPL sessions launched in the SAME cwd (driver start_session step; the 2nd shares session 1's directory, so no trust dialog). The spec asserts the scenario's three user-observable signals: (1) two DISTINCT session_ids appear (s2_birth carries new_session:true); (2) each session has its own independent working→ready arc, pinned by same_session_as so a transition on one can never satisfy the other; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN after session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*), proving its PID-to-session binding stayed stable and did not flap to session 2. Phase order follows the recipe: s1 turn → start_session → s2 turn → s1 turn again.

text: Session 1 appears in ready in the shared cwd
Session 1 first prompt drives ready → working
Session 1 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 2 starts in the SAME cwd as a DISTINCT session_id and appears in ready (new_session proves it is not session 1)
Session 2 first prompt drives ready → working — independent of session 1
Session 2 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive — a working transition on session 1's original id proves the binding stayed stable and did not flap to session 2
Session 1 second turn completes: working → ready
